Pattern: LEFT often removes one, RIGHT often adds one. - b frame split: [28,28]-[31,31] and [28,36]-[31,39]. Gray bar split similarly. - Player cuts frames when moving through them. sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: Let me check what happened after moving LEFT.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed after LEFT
before = last_transition.before_frame.segmentation['nodes']
after = last_transition.after_frame.segmentation['nodes']

print("Before LEFT:")
for n in before:
    if n['color'] not in ['w','W'] and n['boundary'][0][0] < 60:
        bl = n['boundary'][0]
        br = n['boundary'][-1]
        print(f"  {n['color']}: [{bl[0]},{bl[1]}]-[{br[0]},{br[1]}], pixels={n['pixels']}")

print("\nAfter LEFT:")
for n in after:
    if n['color'] not in ['w','W'] and n['boundary'][0][0] < 60:
        bl = n['boundary'][0]
        br = n['boundary'][-1]
        print(f"  {n['color']}: [{bl[0]},{bl[1]}]-[{br[0]},{br[1]}], pixels={n['pixels']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
OK, so after moving LEFT:
1. Player moved from [28,32]-[31,34] to [28,29]-[31,31] (moved left by 3 columns)
2. The c frame at [28,24]-[31,27] converted to G (player moved toward it)
3. The b frame part at [28,28]-[31,31] disappeared (player moved through it)
4. The b frame parts merged: [28,32]-[31,39] (18 pixels) - the right part grew
5. The gray bar parts merged: [29,32]-[30,38] (14 pixels)
6. The c frame at [24,28]-[27,31] is still present
7. The c frame at [28,16]-[31,19] is still present
8. The c frame at [28,24]-[31,27] converted to G
So the player is now at [28,29]-[31,31], adjacent to the G frame at [28,24]-[31,27]. If I move LEFT again, the player would move into the G frame.
Let me try moving LEFT again to see what happens when the player moves into a G frame.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Player is now at [28,29]-[31,31], G frame at [28,24]-[31,27]
# Let me move LEFT into the G frame
action(['LEFT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
